description: Phase A baseline: v0.3-exp03 arch flattened, compile=max-autotune-no-cudagraphs, sdpa_flash, bs=16*32
Pinned-memory dataloader; async H2D copy via pre-allocated pinned buffers
micro_batch 16->32, grad_accum 32->16 — worse tok/s (VRAM pressure), fewer steps, +0.047 bpb
ATTENTION_BACKEND=flash_attn_2: requires pure bf16 Q/K, but RMSNorm weight mul promotes to fp32 under autocast. Would need RMSNorm dtype fix (FROZEN_ARCH-adjacent); skip.
torch.compile(fullgraph=True) — no fusion win at this arch (tok/s -0.7 %, bpb +0.002)
PEAK_LR 6e-4 -> 9e-4: destabilised (bpb +0.052). Stay at 6e-4.
WARMUP_FRAC 0.04 -> 0.02: -0.014 bpb. Fixed-time regime needs less warmup.
MIN_LR_RATIO 0.1 -> 0.2: -0.027 bpb. Cosine tail was too deep for 300-s regime.
MIN_LR_RATIO 0.2 -> 0.3: another -0.020 bpb.
MIN_LR_RATIO 0.3 -> 0.5: another -0.028 bpb. Keep pushing.
MIN_LR_RATIO 0.5 -> 1.0 (constant LR after warmup). -0.034 bpb. Short budget wants no cosine decay.
GRAD_CLIP 1.0 -> 0.0: training destabilised (+0.21 bpb), negligible tok/s win. Keep clipping.
PEAK_LR 6e-4 -> 7.5e-4 (constant LR): +0.023 bpb. 6e-4 is near-optimal for constant-LR.
WARMUP_FRAC 0.02 -> 0.01 (shorter warmup at constant LR): +0.031 bpb. Warmup helps even at constant LR.
pre-compile warmup step before TIME_BUDGET: +0.074 bpb. Compile is disk-cached so warmup=1s; the step ran at PEAK_LR before LR-warmup, destabilising init.
PEAK_LR 6e-4 -> 5e-4 (constant LR regime): -0.007 bpb. Constant LR lifts average LR so peak is slightly too hot.
PEAK_LR 5e-4 -> 4e-4: +0.017 bpb. 5e-4 is near-optimum for constant-LR.
BETA2 0.95 -> 0.98: +0.013 bpb. Longer variance EMA hurts short-budget training.
BETA2 0.95 -> 0.9: +0.006 bpb. 0.95 is optimum.
BETA1 0.9 -> 0.95: +0.068 bpb. Stronger momentum hurts short-budget training.

COMPILE_MODE max-autotune (CUDA graphs): 'accessing tensor output of CUDAGraphs that has been overwritten' during backward — U-Net skips share wte output across blocks, incompatible with cudagraph output tracking even with mark_step_begin.
ATTENTION_BACKEND sdpa_cudnn: +0.003 bpb (within noise), +1k tok/s. Marginal speedup but not strictly better.
WEIGHT_DECAY 0.1 -> 0.05: +0.002 bpb (within noise).
GRAD_ACCUM 32 -> 16 (effective batch 512 -> 256 seqs): -0.197 bpb. Doubled optimizer steps (219 vs 112) dominate small tok/s dip.
GRAD_ACCUM 16 -> 8 (effective batch 256 -> 128 seqs): -0.135 bpb. 446 optim steps vs 219.
GRAD_ACCUM 8 -> 4 (effective batch 128 -> 64 seqs): -0.065 bpb. 889 optim steps.
GRAD_ACCUM 4 -> 2 (effective batch 64 -> 32 seqs): -0.083 bpb. 1752 optim steps.
GRAD_ACCUM 2 -> 1: +0.014 bpb. Gradient noise dominates at batch=16 seqs; 32 is the elbow.
PEAK_LR 5e-4 -> 3e-4 (batch=32): +0.025 bpb. Smaller batch still wants high LR.
PEAK_LR 5e-4 -> 7e-4 (batch=32): +0.002 bpb (noise). 5e-4 still optimum.
MIN_LR_RATIO 1.0 -> 0.1: +0.017 bpb. Constant LR still wins even at 1752 steps.
MICRO_BATCH 16/GA 2 -> 32/GA 1: +0.005 bpb, tok/s -25459759
column11: WEIGHT_DECAY 0.1 -> 0.0: -0.001 bpb (marginal, still under-fit at 1764 steps).

description: EPS 1e-8 -> 1e-10: +0.005 bpb. Sharper Adam hurts slightly.
GRAD_CLIP 1.0 -> 0.5: +0.001 bpb (noise).
WARMUP_FRAC 0.02 -> 0.005: +0.010 bpb. Short warmup still hurts.
MIN_LR_RATIO 1.0 -> 0.5 (batch=32): +0.001 bpb (noise).
ATTENTION_BACKEND sdpa_efficient: +0.004 bpb, tok/s -1.416d5a8a
column11: Check divergence every 50 steps: +0.005 bpb, +6 steps (0.35edef6a
column16: BETA2 0.95 -> 0.98 at batch=32: -0.014 bpb. Noisier small-batch grads benefit from longer variance EMA.

description: BETA2 0.98 -> 0.99: -0.002 bpb. Even longer variance EMA helps small-batch.
BETA2 0.995 -> 0.999: -0.002 bpb. Classic Adam BETA2 wins at small batch.
PEAK_LR 5e-4 -> 6e-4 at BETA2=0.999: -0.003 bpb. Longer EMA allows higher LR.
